_about: Decoding data for Wall Connector Gen 3 alerts as reported by the local API's
current_alerts field. Tesla does not document these codes; on modern firmware
they are numeric IDs (e.g. [27]). Entries here must be VERIFIED before they
are labeled: the reliable method is to open the Tesla app while an alert is
active on your charger — the app names active alerts — and record the code
the local API showed at the same moment. Unverified guesses do not belong in
this file; a wrong label is worse than a raw number.
Each code entry: {label, description, source, verified}.

40.label: High temperature detected
40.description: High temperature detected; charging is limited. The charger reduces current until it cools. Check the faceplate and cable handle for excessive warmth and rule out wiring issues (conductor sizing, terminal torque); in a hot garage this can simply be ambient heat, and it clears on its own once temperatures drop.
40.source: Tesla app > Wall Connector Troubleshooting, confirmed against an active alert on 2026-07-14 (matches the manual's 'High temperature' fault category)

categories.label: Internal error
categories.description: Charging disabled. Power-cycle the breaker for 5 seconds; contact Tesla if it persists.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: Ground fault (earth fault circuit interruption)
categories.description: Unsafe current path detected; charging disabled. Inspect handle, cable, connector, and vehicle charge port for damage or water ingress.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: Earth assurance fault
categories.description: High ground resistance detected; charging disabled. Verify the ground connection and physical connections in the supply.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: High temperature
categories.description: Charging limited or disabled due to heat. Check the faceplate and cable handle for excessive warmth; verify conductor sizing and terminal torque.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: Internet connection lost
categories.description: Online features disabled; charging still works. Check Wi-Fi signal and router; update Wi-Fi settings if credentials changed.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: Power-sharing communication issue
categories.description: Charging reduced. Re-link power-sharing Wall Connectors via the commissioning process.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: Overvoltage / poor grid quality
categories.description: Charging disabled. Verify supply voltage; have an electrician check terminal readings.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes

categories.label: Vehicle overcurrent
categories.description: The vehicle drew more current than allowed. Reduce the vehicle's charge current setting.
categories.source: Tesla Gen 3 Wall Connector manual, Error Codes
